_meta.description: Strict numeric integrity rules with machine actions for the Excel calc agent quality gate.

rules.description: Detects division operations where the divisor is zero.
rules.userMessage: Cannot divide by zero. The denominator in your calculation is zero.

rules.description: Detects logarithm operations applied to zero or negative values.
rules.userMessage: Logarithm is undefined for zero and negative numbers. Found value: {value}.

rules.description: Detects square root operations on negative numbers (real-number domain).
rules.userMessage: Square root is undefined for negative numbers in the real domain. Found value: {value}.

rules.description: Detects modulo operations where the divisor is zero.
rules.userMessage: Modulo by zero is undefined. The divisor must be non-zero.

rules.description: Detects integer values that exceed the 64-bit signed integer range.
rules.userMessage: Result exceeds the 64-bit integer range (±9.2×10¹⁸). Consider using arbitrary-precision arithmetic.

rules.description: Detects exponentiation results that overflow to infinity.
rules.userMessage: Exponentiation overflow: {base}^{exponent} exceeds representable range.

rules.description: Warns when a negative exponent is applied to an integer base, producing a float result where an integer may be expected.
rules.userMessage: Negative exponent on integer base produces a float. Result: {result}.

rules.description: Detects non-numeric values in a column expected to be fully numeric.
rules.checkExpression: any(not isinstance(v, (int, float)) and not pd.isna(v) for v in column)
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' contains non-numeric values ({count} found). Should I coerce them to numbers or exclude them?

rules.description: Detects columns containing a mix of numeric, string, and other types.
rules.checkExpression: len(set(type(v).__name__ for v in column if not pd.isna(v))) > 1
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' has mixed types: {types_found}. Should I unify to a single type?

rules.description: Detects boolean values used in numeric calculations.
rules.checkExpression: any(isinstance(v, bool) for v in column)
rules.userMessage: Boolean values detected in numeric column '{column_name}'. Treating True=1, False=0.

rules.description: Detects date/datetime values used directly in numeric arithmetic without extraction.
rules.checkExpression: any(isinstance(v, (datetime.date, datetime.datetime)) for v in column)
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' contains dates. Which component should I extract for calculation? (year, month, day, epoch_seconds, ordinal)

rules.description: Detects string values that look like numbers (with currency symbols, commas, whitespace).
rules.userMessage: Auto-coercing string values to numbers (stripping currency symbols and formatting).

rules.description: Detects percentage values that may be ambiguously scaled (0-1 vs 0-100).
rules.checkExpression: any(v > 1.0 for v in column) and any(v <= 1.0 for v in column) and column_label_contains_percent
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' contains values in both 0–1 and >1 ranges. Are these percentages in 0–1 or 0–100 scale?

rules.description: Detects negative values in columns that semantically represent counts.
rules.checkExpression: any(v < 0 for v in column) and column_semantic == 'count'
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' represents a count but contains negative values. Counts must be non-negative.

rules.description: Detects probability values outside the valid [0, 1] range.
rules.checkExpression: any(v < 0 or v > 1 for v in column) and column_semantic == 'probability'
rules.userMessage: Probability values must be between 0 and 1. Found out-of-range values in '{column_name}'.

rules.description: Detects variance calculations that yield negative results (mathematically impossible).
rules.userMessage: Variance cannot be negative. Computed value {result} indicates a calculation error.

rules.description: Detects Pearson/Spearman correlation coefficients outside [-1, 1].
rules.userMessage: Correlation coefficient must be between -1 and 1. Computed value {result} indicates a calculation error.

rules.description: Detects ratio calculations where the denominator is negative, which can flip the sign and mislead interpretation.
rules.userMessage: The denominator in your ratio is negative ({denominator}). This may invert the expected sign of the result. Is this intentional?

rules.description: Warns when floating-point values are compared for exact equality.
rules.checkExpression: operation == 'eq' and isinstance(left, float) and isinstance(right, float)
rules.userMessage: Floating-point equality comparison detected. Using np.isclose(a, b, rtol=1e-9) instead of a == b.

rules.description: Detects results with more than 10 significant decimal digits, likely spurious precision.
rules.checkExpression: len(str(result).split('.')[-1]) > 10 if '.' in str(result) else False
rules.userMessage: Result has excessive decimal places. Rounding to 10 significant digits.

rules.description: Warns when subtracting two nearly equal large numbers, causing significant precision loss.
rules.checkExpression: abs(a - b) / max(abs(a), abs(b)) < 1e-12 and abs(a) > 1e6
rules.userMessage: Catastrophic cancellation detected: subtracting two nearly equal large numbers ({a} - {b}). Result may have lost significant precision.

rules.description: Detects subnormal (denormalized) floating-point values that have reduced precision.
rules.userMessage: Subnormal float detected ({value}). Precision is degraded below the normal floating-point minimum.

rules.description: Warns when summing many floating-point values, where naive summation may accumulate rounding errors.
rules.checkExpression: len(values) > 1000 and any(abs(v) > 1e10 for v in values)
rules.userMessage: Summing {count} values with large magnitudes. Using Kahan compensated summation to reduce rounding error.

rules.description: Detects columns or input arrays where every value is NaN or None.
rules.userMessage: All values in '{column_name}' are missing. Cannot perform any calculation.

rules.description: Detects columns where more than 50% of values are missing.
rules.checkExpression: sum(pd.isna(v) for v in column) / len(column) > 0.5
rules.userMessage: More than 50% of values in '{column_name}' are missing ({pct_missing}%). Should I proceed with available data or stop?

rules.description: Detects NaN/None values in the denominator of a division or ratio.
rules.userMessage: The denominator contains missing values. Cannot compute division with NaN/None divisor.

rules.description: Warns when NaN values propagate through chained operations, potentially contaminating results.
rules.userMessage: NaN values are propagating through the calculation chain. {nan_count} intermediate results are NaN.

rules.description: Detects infinite values (±inf) appearing in computation output.
rules.checkExpression: any(math.isinf(v) for v in results if isinstance(v, float))
rules.userMessage: Result contains infinite values. This typically indicates division by zero or overflow in intermediate steps.

rules.description: Detects columns where only one non-missing value remains, making statistical operations meaningless.
rules.checkExpression: sum(not pd.isna(v) for v in column) == 1
rules.userMessage: Only 1 non-missing value in '{column_name}'. Statistical calculations require at least 2 data points. Proceed anyway?

rules.description: Detects columns containing values with different currency symbols (e.g., $ and €).
rules.userMessage: Multiple currency symbols detected in '{column_name}': {symbols}. Please specify which currency to use or provide exchange rates.

rules.description: Detects columns where some values are expressed as decimals (0.05) and others as percentages (5%).
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' mixes decimal (e.g., 0.05) and percentage (e.g., 5%) formats. Which scale should I normalize to?

rules.description: Detects date strings that are ambiguous between DD/MM/YYYY and MM/DD/YYYY.
rules.checkExpression: any(1 <= int(parts[0]) <= 12 and 1 <= int(parts[1]) <= 12 for parts in date_parts)
rules.userMessage: Ambiguous date format detected (e.g., '{sample_value}'). Is this DD/MM/YYYY or MM/DD/YYYY?

rules.description: Detects columns mixing different measurement units (e.g., kg and lbs, cm and inches).
rules.userMessage: Mixed measurement units detected in '{column_name}': {units}. Please specify a target unit for normalization.

rules.description: Detects statistical operations performed on sample sizes below acceptable minimums.
rules.userMessage: Sample size is {n}, below the recommended minimum of 30 for '{operation}'. Results may not be statistically reliable. Proceed anyway?

rules.description: Detects columns with zero variance used in correlation, regression, or standardization.
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' has zero variance (all values are identical: {constant_value}). Cannot compute {operation}.

rules.description: Detects singular or near-singular matrices that cannot be inverted.
rules.userMessage: The matrix is singular or near-singular (condition number: {cond}). Inversion and regression solutions are unreliable.

rules.description: Detects high multicollinearity among predictor variables via Variance Inflation Factor (VIF).
rules.userMessage: High multicollinearity detected (VIF > 10 for columns: {collinear_columns}). Regression coefficients may be unstable.

rules.description: Detects iterative algorithms (e.g., Newton-Raphson, gradient descent) that fail to converge.
rules.userMessage: Algorithm did not converge after {iterations} iterations (residual: {residual}, tolerance: {tolerance}).

rules.description: Detects statistical tests where degrees of freedom drop to zero or below, making the test invalid.
rules.userMessage: Degrees of freedom is {df} (must be > 0). The model has too many parameters for the available data.

rules.description: Detects final computation results that are NaN.
rules.userMessage: The computation result is NaN (Not a Number). This indicates an undefined operation in the calculation chain.

rules.description: Detects final computation results that are positive or negative infinity.
rules.userMessage: The computation result is infinite ({result}). This typically indicates overflow or division by near-zero values.

rules.description: Detects when a computation returns an empty array or dataframe.
rules.userMessage: The computation returned an empty result. All rows may have been filtered out or the input was empty.

rules.description: Warns when the result is orders of magnitude larger than any input value, which may indicate an error.
rules.checkExpression: abs(result) > 1e6 * max(abs(v) for v in inputs if isinstance(v, (int, float)))
rules.userMessage: The result ({result}) is more than 10⁶× larger than any input value. Please verify the calculation is correct.

rules.description: Detects when the result type does not match the expected output type for the operation.
rules.userMessage: Expected result type '{expected_type}' but got '{actual_type}'. The calculation pipeline may have a type error.

rules.description: Detects negative results for metrics that must be non-negative (e.g., RMSE, MAE, standard deviation).
rules.userMessage: {operation} cannot be negative. Computed value {result} indicates a calculation error.

rules.description: Detects intermediate calculations that overflow IEEE 754 double-precision range.
rules.userMessage: Floating-point overflow: intermediate value exceeds ±1.8×10³⁰⁸. Consider rescaling inputs.

rules.description: Detects when a non-zero intermediate result underflows to zero due to limited floating-point precision.
rules.checkExpression: expected_nonzero and result == 0.0 and abs(analytical_result) < sys.float_info.min
rules.userMessage: Floating-point underflow: a non-zero value was rounded to zero. Result precision may be compromised.

rules.description: Detects references to column names that do not exist in the input data.
rules.userMessage: Column '{column_name}' not found in the data. Available columns: {available_columns}.

rules.description: Detects when the input dataframe has zero rows.
rules.userMessage: The input data is empty (0 rows). Cannot perform any calculations.

rules.description: Detects duplicate column names in the input data which cause ambiguous references.
rules.userMessage: Duplicate column names detected: {duplicates}. Which instance should I use for the calculation?
